explanation: This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the STANDBY state to the TRANSITION state when the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in STANDBY).  The property uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot will eventually reach the TRANSITION state if the pilot is not in the STANDBY state. The antecedent (!standby) must be true until the consequent (state_is_TRANSITION) becomes true.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from STANDBY to TRANSITION only when the pilot is not in control.  The property uses the atomic proposition "state_is_STANDBY" to check if the autopilot is currently in STANDBY mode and "state_is_TRANSITION" to check if it transitions to TRANSITION mode. The negation of "standby" indicates that the pilot is not in control. The implication ensures that if the autopilot is in STANDBY and the pilot is not in control, then eventually the autopilot will be in TRANSITION mode.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from STANDBY to TRANSITION when the pilot is not in control.  The property states that if the autopilot is in STANDBY, and the pilot is not in standby (meaning they are not in control), then in the next state, the autopilot will be in TRANSITION. This directly addresses the natural language requirement without unnecessary or incorrect implication.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that the autopilot will eventually transition from the STANDBY state to the TRANSITION state only when the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in STANDBY).  The formula uses the until operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot must remain in STANDBY until the pilot is not in control (!standby). Once this condition is met, the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state (F state_is_TRANSITION).

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from the STANDBY state to the TRANSITION state when the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).  The formula uses the until operator (U) to express the condition that eventually the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state, given that it starts in the STANDBY state and the pilot is not in standby. The implication ensures that only when the pilot is not in standby does the state transition happen.

This LTL property captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from STANDBY to TRANSITION when the pilot is not in control (i.e., not in standby).  The formula G(!standby -> F(state_is_TRANSITION)) asserts that globally, if the pilot is not in standby, then eventually the system will be in the TRANSITION state. This correctly captures the intent of the natural language description.

This ensures that the change of states happens as intended.

The use of U operator is not needed here since we only consider one transition.  The use of the implies operator (->) ensures that this transition only happens when the pilot is not in control.
